basic_info.about: The University of Rwanda is a public collegiate, multi-campus university formed in 2013 through the merger of Rwanda's previously independent public higher education institutions. It is the largest education institution in the country, hosting seven independent colleges across eight campuses.
basic_info.summary: As Rwanda's premier public university, UR offers a wide range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs with a strong focus on STEM, health sciences, and research-driven development in the East African region.
basic_info.affiliations: Association of Commonwealth Universities (ACU), Inter-University Council for East Africa (IUCEA)
basic_info.recognition: World Bank African Centres of Excellence (ACE)
basic_info.campus_area: Multi-campus (8 locations including Huye, Gikondo, Remera, Nyarugenge, Rukara, Rwamagana, Nyagatare, Busogo)

placements.graduate_outcomes_note: Based on national labor force trends for 2025/2026 where service sector employment rose to 44.9% and overall unemployment fell to 12.4%.

placements.placement_highlights: The University of Rwanda shows a strong alignment with national economic growth, specifically in the service sector which now employs nearly 45% of graduates. Recent 2025/2026 data indicates a narrowing gender gap in employment and a significant portion of graduates entering STEM-related roles.

fees.fees_note: Tuition fees at the University of Rwanda remain stable across the requested years, typically 1,600,000 RWF for privately sponsored undergraduate students and 3,000,000 RWF for local/EAC master's students. Additional administrative costs include a one-time registration fee of 60,000 RWF for new students and 35,000 RWF for continuing students.

scholarships_detail.name: Government Sponsorship
scholarships_detail.eligibility: Rwandan citizens meeting specific academic merit and socio-economic criteria

scholarships_detail.name: World Bank ACE Scholarship
scholarships_detail.eligibility: Postgraduate students in African Centres of Excellence (STEM fields)

infrastructure.facility: School of Architecture and Built Environment (SABE) Building
infrastructure.details: Modern 5,600 square meter facility at Nyarugenge campus designed with sustainable elements like natural ventilation and local lava rock; accommodates up to 600 students.
Hosts four World Bank-funded centers focusing on Energy for Sustainable Development, Internet of Things (IoT), Data Science, and Innovative Teaching and Learning in Mathematics and Science.
Composed of six colleges operating across nine campuses throughout Rwanda, including Gikondo, Nyarugenge, Huye, Busogo, Nyagatare, and Rukara.
Ongoing 'One Student, One Laptop' initiative with over 500 laptops distributed and specialized e-learning platforms for module delivery.

hostel_details.type: Mixed (On-campus residences with priority for students with disabilities, first-year females, and international students)

scholarships.name: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
scholarships.eligibility: Academically talented but economically disadvantaged youth from Rwanda or other African countries; under 28 for undergraduate, under 35 for postgraduate.

scholarships.name: UR Solidarity Scholarship Scheme
scholarships.eligibility: Registered UR students in need; includes student-job opportunities with a maximum of 15 hours per week.

scholarships.name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program
scholarships.eligibility: Citizens or nationals of Rwanda with a valid passport; for master's degree programs in the U.S.

scholarships.name: Chevening Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Rwandan citizens with leadership potential for fully funded master's studies in the UK.
